game_name: Donkey Kong Country 2: Diddy's Kong Quest

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Agile, faster movement, primary action character
Higher jump, hair spin glide mechanic, alternative mobility
Revival mechanic when active character hit; switch control between Diddy/Dixie
Primary combat and traversal mechanism
Reptilian hostiles, defeat by jumping or cartwheeling
Propel in any direction faced; essential traversal tool
Mid-level respawn points for lost lives
Optional challenges to earn Kremcoins; gated by obstacles
Charge attack ability; mounted traversal
Break walls, damage enemies, activate switches
Conceal hidden bonus areas; require specific tools or characters
Altered movement physics; aquatic enemy types
Web-shooting ability; puzzle-solving tool
Altered movement momentum; bee enemy encounters
Traversal over hazards; momentum-based control
Underwater combat and wall-breaking in aquatic sections
Automatic traversal with timed jumps; high-speed pacing
Extreme jump height; vertical exploration unlock
Flight ability; egg-spitting projectile
Activate cannons to reach bonus areas
Visibility reduction; atmospheric hazard signaling
Required defeat to progress; pattern-based combat
Bonus area rewards; unlock Lost World access
Final challenge; multi-phase boss encounter
High-difficulty endgame content; true ending locked behind completion

typical_relief_triggers: Safe platform sections with no active enemies
Character swap via DK Barrel when hit
typical_tension_triggers: Approaching level end without sufficient progress
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Cyclical escalation within levels: safe introduction of mechanics → moderate challenge mixing enemies and environmental hazards → dangerous late-stage gauntlets combining multiple mechanics. Per-world rhythm: early worlds establish baseline tension, mid-game introduces hybrid mechanics (underwater + enemies, moving platforms + combat), late-game stacks hazards. Boss fights punctuate world transitions as tension peaks.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Breakable walls (left or right side) and hidden bonus barrel placements signal off-axis secret areas. Character-specific barrels (Diddy-only or Dixie-only activation) gate certain passages. Height variations and upper/lower paths indicate optional exploration.
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Hidden bonus areas masked by breakable castle walls (requires throwable barrel, Rambi charge, or Enguarde swordfish). Bonus barrels concealed by visual camouflage in level geometry. DK Coins placed in non-obvious traversal paths (grapple vines, high jumps via Rattly, flight-only sections via Squawks).

difficulty_curve.early_game: World 1-2 establish Diddy/Dixie core mechanics (jumping, cartwheeling, barrel cannons) in low-hazard environments. Land-based Kremling enemies are basic. Checkpoint placement is frequent.
difficulty_curve.mid_game: World 3-5 introduce hybrid challenges: underwater sections with altered physics, beehive levels with sticky surfaces, grappling vines requiring timing, animal friends with unique mechanics (Squitter web-shooting, Enguarde sword combat). Enemy variety expands (pufferfish, stingrays, bees, vultures). Checkpoint frequency decreases.
difficulty_curve.late_game: World 6-8 stack multiple mechanics per level: fast-moving skull roller coaster with timed jumps, high-altitude grapple sections, dense enemy groupings, limited visibility (fog/storms). Boss fights escalate. Lost World (post-game) combines all mechanics at elevated difficulty with minimal checkpoints.
notable_spikes: World 4 transition: Skull roller coaster introduces automatic high-speed traversal requiring precise timed jumps
World 5: Vertical height challenges unlock via Rattly; extreme jump mechanic changes spatial problem-solving
Krocodile Kore (K. Rool final boss): Multi-phase pattern-based combat with no checkpoint relief
Lost World levels: Dense mechanic stacking with sparse checkpoints; endgame difficulty spike

notable_level_patterns.pattern_name: Linear horizontal progression with vertical exploration
notable_level_patterns.description: Primary left-to-right traversal with optional upper/lower paths gated by animal friends (Rattly for height, Squawks for flight) or specific character abilities (Dixie's hair glide).

Aquatic sections feature reversed jump physics and slower horizontal movement. Enemy types shift to aquatic variants (pufferfish, stingrays). Enguarde transformation provides combat/wall-breaking capability.
Beehive levels with honey-covered platforms reduce movement momentum. Character becomes 'stuck' briefly, requiring adjusted jump timing. Bee enemies attack during traversal.
Vine swing sequences over hazards (spikes, enemies, water) require momentum timing. Character swings in arcs; jumps must release at correct swing apex.
Sequential cannon shots in specific directions to reach otherwise unreachable areas. Combines directional logic with platforming execution.
Fixed-path high-speed ride on skull-shaped track. Player jumps off at timed intervals or rides to track end. Minimal control but high precision required.
Late-game levels (World 6-8) combine 3+ mechanics: underwater + moving platforms, grapple vines + fog visibility loss, roller coaster + cannon sequences.
Each world boss has repeating attack pattern. Player learns sequence, identifies safe windows, executes counter-attack. Boss health tracked; typically 3-5 hit threshold.
